title: Cassilia's Power Fist Is Released
excerpt: Cassilia's Power Fist brings three standalone Power Fist variants, explosive melee progression, Combat Serum support and a reusable framework for future Starfield Power Fist mods.
content:
Cassilia's Power Fist is now released.

This project brings a fully functional Power Fist system to Starfield, built around explosive melee combat, progression mechanics and Cassilia's own combat legacy.

The release includes three standalone Power Fist variants: UC Vanguard Style, Crimson Fleet Style and Argos Extractors Style. Each version connects to a different chapter of Cassilia's past, from her Vanguard service to undercover operations and her early industrial life as a miner.

These are not simple skins. Each Power Fist is a standalone weapon that can be crafted at the Pharmaceutical Lab, and each one contains hidden details for players to discover.

The system grows stronger through combat. As players unlock ranks, the Power Fist gains new explosive chain effects, delayed detonations, particle-style impact effects and finally a Tesla-based main explosion effect.

There may also be something beyond Rank 4. Cassilia only smiled when I asked her about it.

Cassilia Combat Serum is also part of the release. For a short duration, the serum pushes the Power Fist into an overdrive state, allowing it to unleash its full destructive potential. It is powerful, but it should be used wisely.

This mod is also designed as a reusable framework for other mod authors. The ESM framework can be loaded in Creation Kit, duplicated and used as the foundation for future Power Fist-style weapons. I hope it helps the Starfield modding community build many different close-combat creations.

Cassilia's Power Fist is another step forward for the Cassilia Universe project. Future updates may bring new Power Fist models, additional custom effects, new serum types and more Cassilia-themed content.

Punch first. Ask questions later.

title: Cassilia Personal Log-01: About My Favorite UC Power Fist
excerpt: A recovered personal log from Cassilia's brief service with the UC Vanguard, describing the abandoned Power Fist prototype.
content:
I was not Starborn then.

I was only Cassilia.

For a short time, I wore the colors of the UC Vanguard and tried to believe that discipline, orders and polished military reports could make the Settled Systems feel safer.

That was when they chose me for the prototype.

The engineers called it a close-combat amplification platform. The soldiers called it the Power Fist.

The project was ambitious.

The results were terrifying.

The first time I saw it tested, the impact cracked the reinforced target frame and threw the operator backward across the room. Everyone cheered until he tried to stand. His arm was broken in three places.

They said it was a calibration issue.

It was not.

The weapon did exactly what it was built to do. It amplified force beyond the limits of the human body. Most test subjects could manage only a few strikes before the recoil found bone, tendon or nerve. After that, the reports became quiet. Words like unsuitable, unstable and unacceptable risk began replacing words like breakthrough.

I should have walked away.

Instead, I asked for another trial.

There was something in the rhythm of it. The weight. The delay before impact. The way the whole frame had to move with the strike, not against it. It was not a weapon you wore on your hand. It was a weapon that demanded your entire body answer for it.

The engineers watched me after the third test. Then the sixth. Then the twelfth.

No fracture.
No dislocation.
No hesitation.

After that, they stopped calling it impossible.

During one Vanguard operation, we encountered a Terrormorph before the evacuation route was clear. The official record says suppressive fire forced it back long enough for the squad to withdraw.

That is not what happened.

It came through the smoke faster than anything that large should move. I remember one of the soldiers shouting my name. I remember the prototype locking around my arm. I remember the impact more than the fear.

One strike.

The creature left the ground.

For a moment, everyone stopped firing.

Later, the UC denied the incident. I understand why. A weapon that breaks soldiers is a failed project. A weapon that only one person can use is not a weapon at all. It is a liability with a name.

So the Power Fist disappeared into storage, and the reports became myths.

Some Vanguard recruits still tell the story. Most of them think it is an exaggeration. A battlefield rumor. Another heroic lie polished by time.

Let them think that.

Some truths are safer when they sound impossible.

title: Cassilia UC Vanguard Power Fist — Prototype Phase Complete
excerpt: Development of the Cassilia UC Vanguard Power Fist has entered the prototype stage. Core design, model integration and combat effects are now functional in-game.
content:
The next project in the Cassilia Universe is officially underway.

Cassilia's UC Vanguard Power Fist has now reached the prototype phase. Originally designed during Cassilia's service with the United Colonies Vanguard, the weapon was intended to provide overwhelming close-quarters combat power against hostile wildlife and extreme threats.

Current development progress:

* Prototype visual design completed
* 3D model imported into Starfield
* First-person and third-person testing completed
* Initial combat VFX implemented
* Impact effects and energy strike visuals working in-game

According to internal UC records, the project was eventually suspended after repeated field tests resulted in severe arm injuries among standard Vanguard personnel. The force generated by the Power Fist exceeded safe operating limits for ordinary soldiers.

Cassilia, however, continued to use modified prototypes during several classified Vanguard operations.

The current goal is to deliver a weapon that feels powerful, industrial and believable within Starfield's NASA-punk universe while expanding the growing Cassilia storyline.

More updates will be shared as development progresses.

title: Building Personal Logs On A Preview Branch
excerpt: I started moving the portfolio workflow away from local edits and into safer preview branches.
content:
Today I started building the new Personal Logs section for the portfolio.

At first, this website was mostly a place to show my Starfield mods, screenshots and download numbers.

But after working on Cassilia, THE TERMINUS and the Cassilia Universe for a while, I realized that some things are worth recording outside of normal mod pages.

Not everything is a release note.

Sometimes it is a small idea.
Sometimes it is a development problem.
Sometimes it is a player comment that makes the whole project feel more real.

That is why I wanted Personal Logs to feel less official and more personal.

I also moved this work into a preview branch instead of editing the live site directly. After spending too much time pushing local changes and waiting for deployment, this feels like a much better workflow.

The plan is simple:

* build new website features in preview branches
* review them safely
* merge them only when they feel ready

This should make future updates faster, cleaner and less stressful.

Personal Logs is still a small feature, but I think it will become an important part of the site.

It will be a place for modding notes, Cassilia Universe fragments, website updates, and random moments from the road to becoming a better Starfield creator.

title: Cassilia Uniform Reached 3700 Downloads
excerpt: Cassilia's Old Constellation Uniform passed 3700 combined downloads across Nexus Mods and Creations.
content:
Today I noticed that Cassilia's Old Constellation Uniform passed 3700 combined downloads across Nexus Mods and Creations.

When I first made the outfit, I simply wanted to expand Cassilia's story and show what she looked like before becoming Starborn.

Seeing players enjoy the mod and leave positive feedback has been incredibly motivating.

This is only the beginning of the Cassilia Universe project.

title: Building THE TERMINUS
excerpt: THE TERMINUS started as a simple weapon concept and slowly became a complete experience.
content:
THE TERMINUS started as a simple weapon concept.

Over time it grew into a complete experience featuring:

* A new legendary sword
* A custom desert location
* A unique boss encounter
* The story of Cassilia

Looking back, it became much larger than originally planned.

title: Connecting Codex To GitHub
excerpt: Codex is now connected directly to the portfolio repository, making website updates much faster.
content:
Today I connected Codex directly to the portfolio repository.

This should dramatically reduce the amount of time spent manually editing files and deploying updates.

Future website improvements will now be developed through dedicated preview branches before being merged into production.

title: A Mod Collection Included My Work
excerpt: Seeing one of my mods appear in a larger Starfield collection was a very encouraging moment.
content:
Recently another creator added one of my mods to a larger Starfield collection.

Seeing my work appear alongside established creators was a very encouraging moment.

It reminded me that every project starts small.

title: Who Was Cassilia Before Becoming Starborn?
excerpt: Cassilia's old uniform represents a forgotten chapter of her life before becoming Starborn.
content:
Before becoming Starborn, Cassilia was once associated with Constellation.

Her old uniform represents a forgotten chapter of her life.

Many future projects will continue exploring this part of her story and expand the Cassilia Universe.

title: Someone Finally Called It The Cassilia Universe
excerpt: A player's comment made the Cassilia Universe Project feel real for the first time.
content:
A player recently commented:

"Please keep us updated. I can't wait to see where you take the CUP."

That was the first time someone recognized the larger idea behind these mods.

It made me realize that Cassilia is no longer just a character mod.

The Cassilia Universe Project has officially begun.
